title: 10 best herbal teas for gastritis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gastritis are infusions made from various plants and herbs, which have anti-inflammatory, soothing, and healing properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of gastritis. Gastritis is an inflammation of the lining of the stomach, often caused by bacterial infections, stress, or the use of certain medications. Herbal teas such as chamomile, licorice root, marshmallow root, and slippery elm can help reduce inflammation, protect the stomach lining, and promote healing. By drinking these teas, individuals with gastritis may experience relief from symptoms such as pain, bloating, and acid reflux. This, in turn, can improve their quality of life by allowing them to enjoy their meals without discomfort, have better digestion, and sleep better at night.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with gastritis because it contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate the abdominal pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. Furthermore, lemon balm tea can help stimulate the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and nutrient absorption, while also soothing the gastrointestinal tract. Drinking lemon balm tea regularly may help relieve symptoms of gastritis, promote gut health, and support overall digestive wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for gastritis as it can alleviate pain and promote healing. This property is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Antispasmodic: Lemon balm tea can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping, which is beneficial for gastritis as it can alleviate discomfort and pain. This is attributed to the presence of citral and citronellal.

Carminative: Lemon balm tea can help reduce bloating and gas, which is important for gastritis as it can provide relief from symptoms. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils like citral and geraniol.

Antimicrobial: Lemon balm tea has antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach, such as Helicobacter pylori, which is often associated with gastritis. This property is due to the presence of citral and eugenol.

Anxiolytic: Lemon balm tea can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is important for gastritis as stress can exacerbate symptoms. This property is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and eugenol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing gastritis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have gastroprotective effects and can help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the leaves under cold water to remove any impurities.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2 minutes.
Steep the leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea, discard the leaves, and enjoy the herbal lemon balm tea for gastritis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tea helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. Spearmint has been shown to inhibit the growth of H. pylori bacteria, which is a common cause of gastritis. Additionally, spearmint tea can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as bloating, nausea, and abdominal pain. Its calming effect on the gastrointestinal system can promote healthy digestion and provide relief to those suffering from gastritis. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before incorporating spearmint tea or any other herbal remedy into a treatment plan for gastritis.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Spearmint tea can help relieve gas and bloating by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, which is important for gastritis as it can reduce discomfort and pain in the stomach. This property is attributed to the menthol and menthone present in spearmint.

Anti-inflammatory: Spearmint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is a key symptom of gastritis. The rosmarinic acid and flavonoids in spearmint are responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.

Antispasmodic: Spearmint tea can help relax the stomach muscles and reduce spasms, which can provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. The menthol in spearmint is an active constituent that contributes to this antispasmodic effect.

Antimicrobial: Spearmint tea has antimicrobial properties that can help kill harmful bacteria in the stomach, including Helicobacter pylori, which is often associated with gastritis. The carvacrol and limonene in spearmint give it this antimicrobial property.

Antioxidant: Spearmint tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of gastritis. The rosmarinic acid and flavonoids in spearmint are responsible for its antioxidant effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Spearmint leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the stomach lining and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds contain essential oils and menthol, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce pain and discomfort caused by gastritis.
Stems: Spearmint stems also contain menthol and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried spearmint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable drink.
Optional: Add a small amount of honey or lemon to enhance the tea's flavor, if desired.
Sip the herbal spearmint tea slowly, allowing it to soothe gastritis symptoms and promote digestive health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with gastritis because it contains substances that can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. One of the main active compounds in licorice, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to increase the mucus production in the gastrointestinal tract, which can protect the stomach lining from acid and irritants. Additionally, licorice tea has been found to inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a bacteria that is often associated with gastritis. Furthermore, licorice tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gastric inflammation, a primary symptom of gastritis, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Mucosal protective: The mucosal protective effect of licorice tea helps maintain the stomach lining, preventing further damage from gastritis, by activating the prostaglandin synthesis, thanks to glycyrrhizic and glabridin acids.

Antioxidant: Licorice tea's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress in the stomach, which contributes to gastritis, due to the presence of phenolic compounds, including liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tea's antimicrobial properties help control H. pylori infection, a common cause of gastritis, by inhibiting bacterial growth, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and isoflavonoids.

Gastric ulcer healing: The gastric ulcer healing properties of licorice tea promote the healing of stomach ulcers associated with gastritis, owing to the presence of glycyrrhizin and its derivatives, which stimulate mucosal cell proliferation and enhance the production of gastric mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and heal gastritis.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe gastritis symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant contain various antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help protect the stomach lining and alleviate gastritis.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice tea plant contain glycyrrhizic acid, which can help reduce stomach acid production and provide relief from gastritis.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the lining of the stomach, which is often irritated in individuals with gastritis. Additionally, ginger has been shown to help speed up gastric emptying, reducing the amount of time that stomach acids are in contact with the stomach lining. Ginger tea is also known to help alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms associated with gastritis. Furthermore, ginger has been found to inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a bacterium that can contribute to the development of gastritis. Therefore, drinking ginger tea can provide relief from gastritis symptoms and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms. This is due to the presence of gingerols and shogaols.
Pain-relieving: Ginger tea acts as a natural painkiller, alleviating pain and discomfort caused by gastritis, thanks to the active constituent gingerol.
Antiemetic: Ginger tea can help prevent nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of gastritis, as it contains 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol that regulate gastric motility.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in ginger tea helps protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, supporting overall gastric health.

Gastric mucosal protective: Ginger tea helps maintain the mucosal lining of the stomach, preventing the erosion of stomach lining, which is crucial for individuals with gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property are 6-gingerol and zingerone.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tea for gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining.
Leaves: Ginger leaves are used in making medicinal tea for gastritis as they contain compounds that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing discomfort and pain.
Flowers: Ginger flowers are used in making medicinal tea for gastritis due to their antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals.

Seeds: Ginger seeds are used in making medicinal tea for gastritis as they contain compounds that can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Peel and chop 1-2 inches of fresh ginger root, then let it steep in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice to taste, stirring until the honey is fully dissolved.
Optionally, add 1 teaspoon of chamomile flowers or peppermint leaves for added anti-inflammatory benefits.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the ginger and any added herbs.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day as needed for gastritis relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with gastritis because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. Gastritis is often caused by an infection or inflammation in the stomach, and curcumin can help soothe and heal the damaged tissue. Additionally, turmeric tea can help stimulate the production of bile, which is necessary for digestion and can help prevent the buildup of stomach acid that can lead to gastritis. Drinking turmeric tea regularly may also help boost the immune system and protect against future occurrences of gastritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Curcumin, the active compound in turmeric, helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is a key factor in gastritis.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gastritis.
Antibacterial: Turmeric has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help eliminate the H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis.
Gastroprotective: Turmeric can help strengthen the stomach lining and prevent the formation of ulcers, which are a common symptom of gastritis.
Pain-relieving: Curcumin has been found to have pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with gastritis.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem (rhizome) is used for making medicinal tea for gastritis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and heal the stomach lining.
Leaves: Curry leaves, which are the leaves of the turmeric plant, are used in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their potential to reduce stomach acid and aid in digestion.
Flowers: Although not commonly used, the flowers of the turmeric plant can be included in medicinal tea for gastritis, as they may provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds are not typically used in medicinal tea for gastritis, but they do contain curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe the stomach.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of grated fresh turmeric root and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of grated ginger root and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then mix in 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day between meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, one of the main causes of gastritis. It also has antioxidants that can help protect the stomach from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, white tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a bacteria commonly associated with gastritis. Furthermore, white tea is less processed than other teas, which helps to preserve its natural healing properties. Drinking white tea regularly may help alleviate gastritis symptoms and promote overall stomach health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gastric inflammation, providing relief from gastritis symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of white tea helps protect the gastric lining from damage caused by free radicals, supporting gastritis treatment, thanks to its high polyphenol content.
Antibacterial: White tea's antibacterial properties inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a primary cause of gastritis, owing to the catechins and other phenolic compounds.

Gastric ulcer protection: White tea helps prevent gastric ulcers by increasing mucus secretion and promoting mucosal integrity, which is important for gastritis treatment, due to its flavonoids and alkaloids.
Anti-secretory: White tea's anti-secretory properties help regulate gastric acid production, alleviating gastritis symptoms, because of its tannins and polyphenols.
remedy_parts: Leaves: White tea leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe gastritis symptoms.
Buds: The buds of the white tea plant contain high levels of polyphenols, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: White tea rhyzomes have been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help eliminate H. pylori, a common cause of gastritis.
Flowers: White tea flowers contain flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and let it cool down to 175-185°F (80-85°C), as boiling water can damage the tea's delicate properties.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried herbal white tea (such as pai mu tan or silver needle) to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the hot water over the tea leaves and let it steep for 3-5 minutes, depending on the desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent tea leaves.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe the gastritis symptoms. Enjoy up to 3 cups a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe and heal the lining of the stomach. It can also help reduce stomach acid, which can irritate and inflame the stomach, leading to gastritis. Additionally, fennel tea has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membrane of the stomach, helping to prevent damage from stomach acid. The tea is also known to stimulate the production of digestive juices, promoting healthy digestion and reducing the symptoms of gastritis such as bloating, pain, and nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gastritis-related inflammation in the stomach lining, thanks to compounds like anethole, limonene, and flavonoids.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effect of fennel tea helps alleviate gastritis-induced muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, due to the presence of volatile oils such as fenchone and estragole.

Carminative: Fennel tea's carminative properties aid in expelling gas from the digestive tract, providing relief from bloating and discomfort associated with gastritis, attributed to the tea's essential oils.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of fennel tea help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to the healing process of gastritis, owing to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Digestive stimulant: Fennel tea stimulates the production of digestive enzymes and bile secretion, improving overall digestion and nutrient absorption, which can help manage gastritis symptoms, due to its active constituents, including rosmarinic acid and various essential oils.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in volatile oils, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds, making them useful for soothing an irritated stomach and aiding in digestion.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes have a high concentration of anethole, a compound that can help relax gastrointestinal spasms and reduce gastritis-related pain.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain antioxidants that can help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation associated with gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of crushed fennel seeds, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Filter - Strain the mixture to remove the fennel seeds, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Add - Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice for taste (optional).
Test - Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume - Drink the tea slowly, up to three times a day, after meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tea helps with gastritis because it contains anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. The volatile oils found in caraway seeds, such as carvone and limonene, can help relieve symptoms of gastritis, including bloating, abdominal pain, and excess gas. Additionally, caraway tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve digestion and nutrient absorption, further alleviating gastritis symptoms. Furthermore, caraway tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of developing gastritis.
remedy_properties: Caraway tea has anti-inflammatory properties: This can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is a key factor in gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as carvone and limonene.

Caraway tea has antispasmodic properties: This can help relax the stomach muscles, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as carvone and limonene.

Caraway tea has antibacterial properties: This can help eliminate the presence of Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium that can cause gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as carvone and limonene, as well as monoterpenes such as p-cymene and γ-terpinene.

Caraway tea has carminative properties: This can help reduce gas and bloating, which are common symptoms of gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as carvone and limonene, as well as flavonoids such as quercetin and luteolin.

Caraway tea has antioxidant properties: This can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids such as quercetin and luteolin, as well as phenolic acids such as caffeic and ferulic acids.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for gastritis due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce bloating and gas.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the caraway tea plant have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing an irritated stomach lining.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway tea plant contain volatile oils and tannins that can help to reduce inflammation and provide relief from gastritis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the caraway tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating gastritis.

Roots: The roots of the caraway tea plant contain essential oils and other compounds that have been shown to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, making them a popular choice for medicinal tea for gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried caraway seeds and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the caraway seeds.
Allow the tea to steep for 10 minutes to extract the herb's medicinal properties.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the seeds.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day between meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with gastritis because it contains antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the stomach, such as H. pylori, which is often associated with gastritis. Additionally, thyme tea has been shown to stimulate the production of gastric juices, aiding in digestion and preventing the formation of gastric ulcers. Its soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate symptoms such as pain, bloating, and nausea. Drinking thyme tea regularly may help prevent gastritis and promote overall stomach health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gastritis-related inflammation in the stomach lining, thanks to compounds like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Thyme tea's antimicrobial properties fight H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis, due to the presence of thymol and carvacrol.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in thyme tea helps protect the stomach lining from free radical damage and supports overall gastric health.
Pain-relieving: Thyme tea's pain-relieving properties help alleviate gastritis-related pain and discomfort, thanks to the presence of compounds like thymol and carvacrol.

Gastroprotective: Thyme tea's gastroprotective properties help strengthen and protect the stomach lining, reducing the risk of gastritis, due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing gastritis symptoms.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils and flavonoids that can help reduce stomach inflammation and provide relief from gastritis.
Stems: Although less potent than leaves and flowers, thyme stems still contain beneficial compounds that can aid in treating gastritis when used in medicinal tea.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried thyme or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh thyme leaves.
Steep - Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, covered.
Strain - Strain the tea to remove the herbs.
Sweeten - Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Serve - Enjoy the warm tea slowly, up to three times a day after meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with gastritis because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is a common symptom of gastritis. Additionally, oregano tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a type of bacteria that can contribute to the development of gastritis. The tea may also help to stimulate the production of digestive juices, promoting proper digestion and reducing the likelihood of gastritis symptoms. Furthermore, oregano tea is caffeine-free and can help soothe and calm an upset stomach, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tea helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms, due to its active constituents like carvacrol and rosmarinic acid.
Antibacterial: Oregano tea inhibits the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium commonly associated with gastritis, thanks to its thymol and carvacrol content.
Antioxidant: Oregano tea neutralizes free radicals and protects the gastric mucosa from oxidative stress, supported by its high antioxidant capacity from rosmarinic and caffeic acids.
Pain-relieving: Oregano tea alleviates abdominal pain and discomfort caused by gastritis, as its active compounds, such as carvacrol, possess analgesic properties.
Gastroprotective: Oregano tea promotes gastric mucosal integrity and prevents gastric ulcers, due to its gastroprotective agents like oleanolic acid and ursolic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the oregano tea plant are rich in phenols and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe gastritis.
Flowers: The flowers of the oregano tea plant contain antioxidants that can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation associated with gastritis.

Seeds: The seeds of the oregano tea plant are a good source of essential oils, such as thymol and carvacrol, which have antibacterial properties that can help eliminate H. pylori, a common cause of gastritis.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried oregano leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the oregano leaves.
Allow the tea to cool to a drinkable temperature before consuming.
